entry.title: Re: Concern about "Inscriptions"
entry.summary:
The email provides insights into a project named STAMPS, which is designed to handle image data by dividing it into chunks and embedding these chunks into bare multisig outputs. This particular method of handling data is less efficient in terms of space utilization as it leads to an expansion of the UTXO set when compared to integrating data within a witness structure.
Further detailed in the discussion is an alternative approach known as Pay-to-Contact, which can be explored through a dedicated resource provided [here](https://bitcoinops.org/en/topics/pay-to-contract-outputs/:~:text=Pay%2Dto%2Dcontract%20protocols%20allow,that%20commits%20to%20that%20text). This technique allows for modifications to a public key with a small piece of data. Consequently, this enables the production of a signature that verifies the possession of the corresponding private key. An example transaction utilizing the discussed technology can be viewed on Mempool at the following URL: [Mempool Transaction Example](https://mempool.space/tx/ee9ed76fa2318deb63a24082a8edc73e4ea39a5252bfb1c1e1c02bd02c52f95f). This example helps illustrate the practical application and implications of the STAMPS project's approach to handling data within blockchain frameworks.
